summary: Grainy security/CCTV-style footage of a crowded JFK airport checkpoint where an ICE/TSA officer performs a Heimlich maneuver on an unresponsive infant. It is a single fixed-camera surveillance angle of the chaotic scene (TSA-vested staff, travelers, baggage carts) with no graphics, titles, color grading, or music-video styling. A human-interest hero story told with raw footage, not an entertainment/genre-styled produced piece, so it is out of scope.
notable_text: Tweet describes ICE officer reviving an infant who stopped breathing at JFK; on-frame 'TSA' visible on staff vests
